title: top: make output tabular
body:
Make the output of top tabular to be compatible with Docker.

Fixes: #458
Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <vrothberg@suse.com>

comments.body: @baude @mheon I believe there was a reason why we did not do this?

@rhatdan Based on #458, the main concern was that some columns can contain whitespaces, so I had a look at what Moby does (as mentioned here https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/issues/458#issuecomment-396838642) and it turns out that the  basic assumption is that `CMD` is always the last column and all exceeding ones get merged into that.
Can we guarantee that `CMD` is the last entry? Can we validate that it is somewhere? Because if we can, I'd be happy to get this in, the `top` API is really gross right now.

> Can we guarantee that CMD is the last entry? Can we validate that it is somewhere? Because if we can, I'd be happy to get this in, the top API is really gross right now.

Yes, we can guarantee it as we specify the ps output explicitly just as Moby is doing as well.
I am fully in favor of forcing some structure on the arguments we pass into `ps`.
@vrothberg Can we do a check for this in `GetContainerPidInformation` though, to make sure that if `cmd` exists in the args list passed in it's the last element?

@mheon I would personally prefer to just hard-code the arguments into `GetContainerPidInformation`. If a user wants a different output, then there is still the option to `exec`. The main issue is that `ps` doesn't have a very friendly output format as grep for instance, where we can split columns with \0.
I agree lets remove the flexibility here and force the users to exec into the container to execute the ps command.
great work @vrothberg !  thanks for doing this ... LGTM
I will leave this up to @mheon and @vrothberg to figure out his command about CMD.  ANd then merge.

I just updated the PR. It took a bit longer but I wanted to have a closer look at Moby. @mheon and I have agreed on ignoring the argument in `GetContainerPidInformation(args []string)` and just hard-code the ps argument until we have figured out a more generic and reliable way of extracting the data.

The basic problem is that ps doesn't support a way of splitting columns in a way to reliably parse the output and Moby/Docker seems to struggle as well. @mheon and I were dreaming about a library in go that does all the procFS parsing, but there is none yet (to the best of my knowledge). I may find some time next month during Hackweek to look at it.

Also I changed the ps args to `-ef` as Moby does. I noticed that the UID in docker-top reports root, which doesn't work with the `uid` format flag which always uses the numerical ID.

:pushpin: Commit 1b9c608 has been approved by `rhatdan`
